subject: Appointment confirmation - Dr. Anita Singh, Feb 11
snippet: This confirms your rheumatology appointment with Dr. Singh on February 11...
body:
Dear Mr. Roberts,

This confirms your rheumatology appointment with Dr. Anita Singh on Wednesday, February 11, 2027 at 2:00 PM, including a uric acid blood draw. Please arrive 15 minutes early and continue your allopurinol as prescribed.

Naperville Rheumatology Associates

subject: Re: Appointment confirmation - Dr. Anita Singh, Feb 11
snippet: Thank you, the time works. I will plan to come in fasting for the draw...
body:
Thank you, the time works well. I will plan to come in for the 2:00 appointment and keep up the allopurinol in the meantime. Please let me know if anything needs to change.

Kind regards,
Andrew Roberts

subject: Your recent lab results are available
snippet: Your A1c result from your recent visit is now available in the portal...
body:
Dear Mr. Roberts,

Your A1c result from your October visit is now available in the patient portal. Dr. Ivers has reviewed it and noted continued improvement at 5.7%. His recommendation is to stay the course with diet and walking. No medication change is needed.

DuPage Family Medicine

subject: Sunday dinner + a couple photos of the kids
snippet: Pot roast Sunday, the usual 5pm. Lily made the soccer team...
body:
Hi Dad,

We are on for Sunday at 5 as usual - pot roast. Lily made the travel soccer team, so brace yourself for a full report. Sam wants to show you something he built. A couple of photos attached.

Love,
Meg

subject: Re: Sunday dinner + a couple photos of the kids
snippet: Wonderful news about Lily. Tell Sam I want the full engineering tour...
body:
That is wonderful news about Lily - please tell her I am proud. And tell Sam I expect the full engineering tour of whatever he has built. I will bring the good bread. See you at 5.

Dad

subject: Re: Sunday dinner + a couple photos of the kids
snippet: Perfect. Phil says hi and asks if you have looked at the tax folder...
body:
Perfect. Phil says hi and asks whether you have had a chance to look at the tax folder he shared - no rush, just before year end. See you Sunday.

Meg

subject: Bears Sunday
snippet: Caught the late game? Talk at 10 as usual on Sunday...
body:
Dad - that fourth quarter was something. We still on for the usual 10 o'clock call Sunday? Sarah says hello. I will be down for the weekend of the 14th next month.

Tom

subject: Re: Bears Sunday
snippet: Painful but I watched every minute. 10 o'clock works...
body:
Painful, but I watched every minute. Ten o'clock works. Looking forward to having you and Sarah down on the 14th - I will get the spare room ready.

Dad

subject: Thursday crew + November newsletter
snippet: Thanks for Thursday. Could you draft the 'then and now' newsletter item?...
body:
Hi Andrew,

Thank you for another solid Thursday morning - the box of Prairieview ledgers you sorted was a real find. Would you be willing to draft the 'Then and Now' item for the November member newsletter? Your eye for the industrial history is exactly right for it.

Linda

subject: Re: Thursday crew + November newsletter
snippet: Happy to. I will have a draft to you by next Thursday...
body:
Happy to take that on. I will have a draft to you by next Thursday for review - I would like to use one of the foundry photographs if Margaret can pull the negative. Glad the ledgers were useful.

Andrew

subject: Re: Thursday crew + November newsletter
snippet: I can scan the foundry negatives Thursday if that helps...
body:
Andrew - I can have the foundry negatives scanned by Thursday if that helps your draft. Good seeing you this week.

Frank

subject: Quarterly review - a few minutes by phone?
snippet: Your statements are ready. Allocation is holding steady; want to talk?...
body:
Andrew,

Your third-quarter statements are posted. The conservative allocation is holding up well and nothing needs changing. If you would like, we can spend ten minutes on the phone next week so you have it for your monthly review. No action required on your part.

Paul Morrow
Edward Jones

subject: Re: Quarterly review - a few minutes by phone?
snippet: Thank you, Paul. Tuesday after 1pm would suit me...
body:
Thank you, Paul. Tuesday after one o'clock would suit me - I have my class and coffee with Ed in the morning. Glad to hear it is steady.

Andrew

subject: Your Etsy order from SpenglerClockParts is confirmed
snippet: Order confirmed: brass suspension spring and verge for mantel clock...
body:
Hi Andrew,

Your order from SpenglerClockParts is confirmed:
- Brass suspension spring (Hermle) x1
- Verge and crutch assembly x1
Order total: $34.80. You will get a shipping notice when it is on the way.

Thanks for shopping on Etsy.

subject: Your order has shipped
snippet: Shipped via USPS. Tracking included. Let me know how the bushing fits...
body:
Andrew,

Your parts went out today via USPS. The suspension spring is the correct gauge for that Hermle movement. Let me know how the verge seats - happy to swap if the depthing is off.

Carl, Spengler Clock Parts

subject: UPS: Your package is on the way
snippet: Your package from a bookseller is scheduled for delivery...
body:
Hello Andrew Roberts,

A package is on the way to 1528 Heatherton Lane. Scheduled delivery: Thursday by end of day. Reference: a hardcover order. Track for the latest status.

UPS

subject: UPS: Your package was delivered
snippet: Delivered to front door at 2:14 PM...
body:
Hello Andrew Roberts,

Your package was delivered to the front door at 1528 Heatherton Lane on Thursday at 2:14 PM. Thank you for using UPS.

subject: Workshop - bring a one-page memory next week
snippet: Lovely work last session. Next week bring a one-page childhood memory...
body:
Dear Andrew,

Your piece on the workbench was the strongest in the room last week - spare and true. For next session, please bring a one-page memory from childhood; we will work on compression. No need to polish it.

Warmly,
Susan Kettering
Naperville Public Library

subject: Re: Workshop - bring a one-page memory next week
snippet: Thank you - I will bring one. Compression is the hard part...
body:
Thank you, Susan - that is kind, and a little mortifying. I will bring a page. Compression is the hard part for an engineer; we are trained to add the caveats.

Andrew

subject: Il Forno on the 14th?
snippet: Are we still on for the 14th? Six-thirty? The fish special is back...
body:
Andrew,

Are we still on for Il Forno on the 14th? Six-thirty? I am told the grilled fish special is back. I will be ready when you get to Wheaton - and I found a book I think you will argue with, which is the best kind.

Janet

subject: Re: Il Forno on the 14th?
snippet: We are. I will pick you up at 5:45. Bring the argument...
body:
We are, and I will be at your door at quarter to six. The fish special is reason enough; the book you want me to argue with is the rest. Looking forward to it.

Andrew

subject: Your hold is ready for pickup
snippet: The Ironworks of Lake County is ready at the Naperville Public Library...
body:
Hello Andrew,

The item you requested - 'The Ironworks of Lake County' by Harold Olsen - is ready for pickup at the Naperville Public Library. It will be held until October 15.

Naperville Public Library

subject: You have been selected
snippet: Congratulations you may have won a cruise...
body: Congratulations! You may have already won a luxury cruise. Click now to claim within 24 hours.

snippet: Thank you, the time works. I will plan to come in fasting for the draw...
body:
Thank you, the time works well. I will plan to come in for the 2:00 appointment and keep up the allopurinol in the meantime. Please let me know if anything needs to change.

Kind regards,
Andrew Roberts

subject: Your recent lab results are available
snippet: Your A1c result from your recent visit is now available in the portal...
body:
Dear Mr. Roberts,

Your A1c result from your October visit is now available in the patient portal. Dr. Ivers has reviewed it and noted continued improvement at 5.7%. His recommendation is to stay the course with diet and walking. No medication change is needed.

DuPage Family Medicine

subject: Sunday dinner + a couple photos of the kids
snippet: Pot roast Sunday, the usual 5pm. Lily made the soccer team...
body:
Hi Dad,

We are on for Sunday at 5 as usual - pot roast. Lily made the travel soccer team, so brace yourself for a full report. Sam wants to show you something he built. A couple of photos attached.

Love,
Meg

subject: Re: Sunday dinner + a couple photos of the kids
snippet: Wonderful news about Lily. Tell Sam I want the full engineering tour...
body:
That is wonderful news about Lily - please tell her I am proud. And tell Sam I expect the full engineering tour of whatever he has built. I will bring the good bread. See you at 5.

Dad

subject: Re: Sunday dinner + a couple photos of the kids
snippet: Perfect. Phil says hi and asks if you have looked at the tax folder...
body:
Perfect. Phil says hi and asks whether you have had a chance to look at the tax folder he shared - no rush, just before year end. See you Sunday.

Meg

subject: Bears Sunday
snippet: Caught the late game? Talk at 10 as usual on Sunday...
body:
Dad - that fourth quarter was something. We still on for the usual 10 o'clock call Sunday? Sarah says hello. I will be down for the weekend of the 14th next month.

Tom

subject: Re: Bears Sunday
snippet: Painful but I watched every minute. 10 o'clock works...
body:
Painful, but I watched every minute. Ten o'clock works. Looking forward to having you and Sarah down on the 14th - I will get the spare room ready.

Dad

subject: Thursday crew + November newsletter
snippet: Thanks for Thursday. Could you draft the 'then and now' newsletter item?...
body:
Hi Andrew,

Thank you for another solid Thursday morning - the box of Prairieview ledgers you sorted was a real find. Would you be willing to draft the 'Then and Now' item for the November member newsletter? Your eye for the industrial history is exactly right for it.

Linda

subject: Re: Thursday crew + November newsletter
snippet: Happy to. I will have a draft to you by next Thursday...
body:
Happy to take that on. I will have a draft to you by next Thursday for review - I would like to use one of the foundry photographs if Margaret can pull the negative. Glad the ledgers were useful.

Andrew

subject: Re: Thursday crew + November newsletter
snippet: I can scan the foundry negatives Thursday if that helps...
body:
Andrew - I can have the foundry negatives scanned by Thursday if that helps your draft. Good seeing you this week.

Frank

subject: Quarterly review - a few minutes by phone?
snippet: Your statements are ready. Allocation is holding steady; want to talk?...
body:
Andrew,

Your third-quarter statements are posted. The conservative allocation is holding up well and nothing needs changing. If you would like, we can spend ten minutes on the phone next week so you have it for your monthly review. No action required on your part.

Paul Morrow
Edward Jones

subject: Re: Quarterly review - a few minutes by phone?
snippet: Thank you, Paul. Tuesday after 1pm would suit me...
body:
Thank you, Paul. Tuesday after one o'clock would suit me - I have my class and coffee with Ed in the morning. Glad to hear it is steady.

Andrew

subject: Your Etsy order from SpenglerClockParts is confirmed
snippet: Order confirmed: brass suspension spring and verge for mantel clock...
body:
Hi Andrew,

Your order from SpenglerClockParts is confirmed:
- Brass suspension spring (Hermle) x1
- Verge and crutch assembly x1
Order total: $34.80. You will get a shipping notice when it is on the way.

Thanks for shopping on Etsy.

subject: Your order has shipped
snippet: Shipped via USPS. Tracking included. Let me know how the bushing fits...
body:
Andrew,

Your parts went out today via USPS. The suspension spring is the correct gauge for that Hermle movement. Let me know how the verge seats - happy to swap if the depthing is off.

Carl, Spengler Clock Parts

subject: UPS: Your package is on the way
snippet: Your package from a bookseller is scheduled for delivery...
body:
Hello Andrew Roberts,

A package is on the way to 1528 Heatherton Lane. Scheduled delivery: Thursday by end of day. Reference: a hardcover order. Track for the latest status.

UPS

subject: UPS: Your package was delivered
snippet: Delivered to front door at 2:14 PM...
body:
Hello Andrew Roberts,

Your package was delivered to the front door at 1528 Heatherton Lane on Thursday at 2:14 PM. Thank you for using UPS.

subject: Workshop - bring a one-page memory next week
snippet: Lovely work last session. Next week bring a one-page childhood memory...
body:
Dear Andrew,

Your piece on the workbench was the strongest in the room last week - spare and true. For next session, please bring a one-page memory from childhood; we will work on compression. No need to polish it.

Warmly,
Susan Kettering
Naperville Public Library

subject: Re: Workshop - bring a one-page memory next week
snippet: Thank you - I will bring one. Compression is the hard part...
body:
Thank you, Susan - that is kind, and a little mortifying. I will bring a page. Compression is the hard part for an engineer; we are trained to add the caveats.

Andrew

subject: Il Forno on the 14th?
snippet: Are we still on for the 14th? Six-thirty? The fish special is back...
body:
Andrew,

Are we still on for Il Forno on the 14th? Six-thirty? I am told the grilled fish special is back. I will be ready when you get to Wheaton - and I found a book I think you will argue with, which is the best kind.

Janet

subject: Re: Il Forno on the 14th?
snippet: We are. I will pick you up at 5:45. Bring the argument...
body:
We are, and I will be at your door at quarter to six. The fish special is reason enough; the book you want me to argue with is the rest. Looking forward to it.

Andrew

subject: Your hold is ready for pickup
snippet: The Ironworks of Lake County is ready at the Naperville Public Library...
body:
Hello Andrew,

The item you requested - 'The Ironworks of Lake County' by Harold Olsen - is ready for pickup at the Naperville Public Library. It will be held until October 15.

Naperville Public Library
